title: Sinners
description: Annie's voiceover introduces the legend of musicians who can pierce the veil between life and death, attracting both healing and evil, illustrated by various woodcuts. The scene then shifts to 1932 Clarksdale, Mississippi, where an Injured Man seeks refuge at Bert and Joan's farmhouse, claiming to be pursued by Choctaw. Despite Joan's misgivings, Bert lets him in for gold, only for the Injured Man to kill Bert and reanimate him as a demonic entity. The scene concludes by introducing Smoke and Stack at an abandoned lumber mill.

C-SIN-01.name: Annie

K-SIN-001.content: Annie possesses knowledge of ancient legends about musicians who can bridge life and death.

K-SIN-002.content: Annie believes this musical gift can attract evil entities.

K-SIN-112.content: Annie runs a shop that includes a Tarot card reading table and sells pickled items in mason jars.

K-SIN-113.content: Annie sells 'High John' dust and accepts 'Plantation Currency' as payment.

K-SIN-114.content: Annie believes in the power of roots and magic, praying and working them to keep Smoke and Stack safe.

K-SIN-115.content: Annie has experienced the loss of a baby with Smoke, a shared trauma that remains fresh.

K-SIN-116.content: Annie knows how to 'feed' a mojo bag, indicating knowledge of spiritual practices.

K-SIN-122.content: Annie is capable of intense physical and emotional intimacy, using it to reconnect with Smoke.

K-SIN-147.content: Annie believes that customers need to feel 'wanted' for the juke joint to succeed.

K-SIN-202.content: Annie is empathetic and caring, especially towards Mary.

K-SIN-241.content: Annie is decisive and takes charge in a crisis, prioritizing the safety of the group.

K-SIN-242.content: Annie believes the attacker was a 'haint' or something worse, requiring specific actions like moving the body.

K-SIN-243.content: Annie suspects Cornbread might be a 'haint' due to his suspicious behavior and timing.

K-SIN-254.content: Annie has knowledge of vampire weaknesses, specifically the efficacy of pickled garlic.

K-SIN-255.content: Annie believes that once a person is turned into a vampire, they cannot be brought back without evil means.

K-SIN-256.content: Annie is willing to perform a mercy killing on a bitten ally to prevent them from turning into a vampire.

K-SIN-257.content: Annie can read bones for divination, though the results can be unsettling.

K-SIN-290.content: Annie discovers that the vampires feel Remmick's pain, causing them to recoil when he is hurt.

K-SIN-291.content: Annie is bitten by Stack and is dying.

C-SIN-01.imagePrompt: A woman wearing white, nursing an infant.

C-SIN-02.name: Injured Man

K-SIN-003.content: The Injured Man is a demonic entity with red eyes and the ability to reanimate the dead.

K-SIN-004.content: The Injured Man uses deception and violence to achieve his goals.

K-SIN-005.content: The Injured Man is pursued by Choctaw, who recognize his dangerous nature.

C-SIN-02.imagePrompt: A man with burns and bleeding wounds, appearing tortured, later revealing red eyes and a toothy grin.

C-SIN-03.name: Bert

K-SIN-006.content: Bert is a musician who plays the guitar and hums tunes.

K-SIN-007.content: Bert is a member of the Ku Klux Klan, possessing a uniform and weapons.

K-SIN-008.content: Bert is susceptible to greed, willing to compromise safety for gold.

K-SIN-009.content: Bert is killed and reanimated by the Injured Man, becoming a demonic entity.

K-SIN-285.content: Bert is Sammie's uncle and betrayed the Juke Joint by selling it to Remmick's group.

K-SIN-286.content: Bert was complicit in the plan to rob and kill everyone at the Juke Joint.

C-SIN-03.imagePrompt: A man who is complicit in the betrayal, nodding in agreement with Remmick.

C-SIN-04.name: Joan

K-SIN-010.content: Joan is skeptical and cautious, especially regarding strangers and unusual remedies.

K-SIN-011.content: Joan is capable of using a rifle for defense.

K-SIN-012.content: Joan is protective of her home and Bert.

K-SIN-198.content: Joan is assertive and quick to challenge authority when denied entry.

K-SIN-213.content: Joan is capable of ambushing individuals in the dark and extinguishing their light source.

C-SIN-04.imagePrompt: A woman who is part of Remmick's group.

C-SIN-05.name: CHAYTON

K-SIN-013.content: Chayton is a leader of a Choctaw search party.

K-SIN-014.content: Chayton is pursuing a dangerous individual and is determined to capture him.

K-SIN-015.content: Chayton possesses an understanding of the Injured Man's true nature.

C-SIN-05.imagePrompt: A Choctaw man in his 50s, leading a search party, wearing practical clothing suitable for travel.

C-SIN-06.name: Lookout
C-SIN-06.imagePrompt: A member of the Choctaw search party, dismounted from a horse, observing the setting sun.

C-SIN-07.name: Smoke

K-SIN-016.content: Smoke is an identical twin, older than Stack.

K-SIN-017.content: Smoke is observant, watching the road ahead.

K-SIN-024.content: Smoke is unflinching and assertive when confronting Hogwood, demonstrating a strong will.

K-SIN-025.content: Smoke is willing to use threats of violence to secure his interests and establish boundaries.

K-SIN-054.content: Smoke is protective of Sammie, inquiring about his father's treatment and his general well-being.

K-SIN-055.content: Smoke is pragmatic and cautious, preferring to delay plans if conditions are not ideal.

K-SIN-056.content: Smoke is capable of quickly and decisively killing a dangerous animal.

K-SIN-057.content: Smoke believes that Chicago, despite its reputation, is not fundamentally different from the South in terms of racial prejudice.

K-SIN-069.content: Smoke is known as one of the 'Smokestack Twins'.

K-SIN-070.content: Smoke is willing to pay people for simple tasks, negotiating the price.

K-SIN-072.content: Smoke is willing to shoot people who attempt to rob him, even acquaintances.

K-SIN-073.content: Smoke believes people from Maybell plantation should have a limp if they almost robbed him.

K-SIN-093.content: Smoke is willing to pay for services and has a significant amount of cash.

K-SIN-107.content: Smoke carries flowers he bought from Grace and Bo Chow.

K-SIN-108.content: Smoke and Stack bought an old saw mill outside of Clarksdale with the intention of turning it into a juke joint.

K-SIN-109.content: Smoke believes all money comes with blood, reflecting a cynical and pragmatic worldview.

K-SIN-110.content: Smoke does not believe in roots, ghosts, demons, or magic, asserting a materialist perspective.

K-SIN-111.content: Smoke carries a leather necklace with his dog tag and a small leather bag, which Annie refers to as a mojo bag.

K-SIN-123.content: Smoke still feels pain returning to Annie's shop, indicating unresolved emotional baggage.

K-SIN-124.content: Smoke is highly responsive to Annie's physical advances, showing deep lingering desire.

K-SIN-145.content: Smoke prioritizes strict cash-only business practices, rejecting non-standard currency.

K-SIN-146.content: Smoke is resistant to compromising business principles for customer sentiment.

K-SIN-180.content: Smoke is surprised by Sammie's musical talent, indicating he was unaware of its depth.

K-SIN-181.content: Smoke is responsible for tallying the till and managing the juke's finances.

K-SIN-193.content: Smoke is quick to suspect outsiders, especially those who might be associated with the Klan.

K-SIN-194.content: Smoke is protective of Sammie, stepping in front of him when Remmick shows interest.

K-SIN-221.content: Smoke believes Mound Bayou is a safe haven for Black people, founded by freed slaves, with no crime or 'crackas'.

K-SIN-222.content: Smoke is capable of extreme violence to maintain order and protect his family, as shown by his actions against Gambler 1 and Mary.

K-SIN-223.content: Smoke believes his father was a hateful man who would have killed Stack, justifying his own act of patricide as protection.

K-SIN-224.content: Smoke is fiercely protective of Stack, prioritizing his brother's safety above all else.

K-SIN-244.content: Smoke is consumed by grief and anger over his brother's death, making him irrational.

K-SIN-245.content: Smoke believes Stack's body should remain with him, rejecting practical advice to move it.

K-SIN-246.content: Smoke views Stack as 'the best thing about me,' indicating a deep emotional dependence.

K-SIN-258.content: Smoke is protective of his brother, Stack, even when confronted with evidence of his transformation.

K-SIN-259.content: Smoke is willing to kill a threat to protect himself and others.

K-SIN-269.content: Smoke is willing to use violence to enforce his will and protect the group, even against allies.

K-SIN-301.content: Smoke's Mojo bag protects him from vampire bites, indicating a magical defense.

K-SIN-302.content: Smoke killed his own father to save Stack, revealing a traumatic and morally complex past action.

K-SIN-312.content: Smoke carries deep emotional scars, evidenced by his shaking hands and welling eyes when attempting to roll a cigarette.

K-SIN-313.content: Smoke is capable of extreme violence and strategic planning when provoked, as shown by his ambush and assembly of weapons.

K-SIN-314.content: Smoke experiences visions or hallucinations of Annie and an infant, suggesting a profound internal conflict or longing.

C-SIN-07.imagePrompt: A man with a weathered face, strong build, and determined eyes.

C-SIN-08.name: Stack

K-SIN-018.content: Stack is an identical twin, younger than Smoke.

K-SIN-019.content: Stack smokes cigarettes and shares them with his brother.

K-SIN-026.content: Stack is willing to use deception, claiming to be cousins with Smoke to avoid revealing their true relationship.

K-SIN-027.content: Stack is prepared to use money to influence local power structures, specifically the Klan.

K-SIN-058.content: Stack is more impulsive and optimistic than Smoke, eager to proceed with plans quickly.

K-SIN-059.content: Stack is willing to share sensitive family secrets, such as the circumstances of Adam's death.

K-SIN-060.content: Stack believes that racial prejudice in the North is more complex and varied than in the South.

K-SIN-061.content: Stack believes his father, Adam, was mostly good but became abusive when drunk.

K-SIN-101.content: Stack experiences genuine fear for the first time, revealing a vulnerability previously unseen.

K-SIN-102.content: Stack is deeply affected by Mary's words, showing he is cut by her revelations and accusations.

K-SIN-103.content: Stack lies about his past relationship with Mary's mother, indicating a desire to conceal his true feelings or history.

K-SIN-125.content: Stack is willing to give explicit and crude advice on sexual matters to Sammie.

K-SIN-126.content: Stack believes that emotional connection and respect are key to keeping a woman, beyond physical acts.

K-SIN-127.content: Stack is willing to use aggressive and disrespectful language to motivate or recruit others.

K-SIN-140.content: Stack is willing to bend business rules for customer satisfaction and community building.

K-SIN-167.content: Stack is one of the Twins, who are Sammie's cousins and owners of the Lumber Mill.

K-SIN-168.content: Stack previously cared about Sammie and Smoke.

K-SIN-169.content: Stack taught Mary how to fight and how to leverage her appearance for financial gain.

K-SIN-170.content: Stack believes Mary should return to her rich white husband and farm to avoid danger.

K-SIN-174.content: Stack believes his past actions of leaving Mary were to ensure her safety.

K-SIN-175.content: Stack thinks about Mary every day, indicating lingering affection and regret.

K-SIN-176.content: Stack feels pride in Sammie's musical performance.

K-SIN-191.content: Stack is visibly stressed by the Juke Joint's financial difficulties.

K-SIN-192.content: Stack carries a Luger pistol, which he is willing to entrust to Mary for protection.

K-SIN-214.content: Stack is anxious and easily scared, especially when his pistol is light.

K-SIN-215.content: Stack is aroused by Mary's suggestive behavior and drooling.

K-SIN-216.content: Stack is capable of robbing trains and banks.

K-SIN-228.content: Stack was the one who suggested going to Mound Bayou.

K-SIN-229.content: Stack is in a romantic or sexual relationship with Mary.

K-SIN-230.content: Stack has been bitten by Mary on the jugular vein.

K-SIN-238.content: Stack is dead, his eyes open but empty, after convulsing from a gunshot wound.

K-SIN-251.content: Stack is a vampire, capable of mimicking human voices and appearances to deceive victims.

K-SIN-253.content: Stack possesses immense physical strength, capable of breaking down a sturdy door with kicks.

K-SIN-287.content: Stack can see memories, confirming Bert's betrayal.

K-SIN-288.content: Stack believes that joining Remmick is the 'only way' to achieve their dream of freedom.

K-SIN-289.content: Stack is willing to bite Annie, demonstrating his commitment to Remmick's cause.

K-SIN-299.content: Stack is Smoke's brother, revealing a deep, familial history between them.

K-SIN-300.content: Stack believes Smoke killed their father to save him, indicating a complex and painful shared past.

K-SIN-328.content: Stack is alive and well in 1992, appearing with Mary at Sammie's bar.

K-SIN-331.content: Stack is a supernatural being who does not cast a reflection in mirrors.

K-SIN-332.content: Stack has the capability to offer immortality to others.

K-SIN-333.content: Stack made a promise to his brother to stay away from Sammie.

C-SIN-08.imagePrompt: A man wearing sunglasses, with a lean build.

K-SIN-037.content: Sammie feels constrained by his family's expectations and desires personal freedom.

K-SIN-049.content: Sammie is eager to experience life beyond his current home and is curious about the wider world, particularly Chicago.

K-SIN-050.content: Sammie is willing to defy his father's warnings to pursue his own path.

K-SIN-051.content: Sammie is capable of playing the guitar well, indicating a developed musical skill.

K-SIN-066.content: Sammie possesses an innate, God-given command over the guitar that cannot be taught.

K-SIN-132.content: Sammie is thoughtful and internalizes Stack's advice on treating women.

K-SIN-133.content: Sammie is deeply affected by the harsh realities of racial injustice, as evidenced by his reaction to Rice's lynching.

K-SIN-141.content: Sammie is observant and eager to learn musical skills from experienced musicians.

K-SIN-152.content: Sammie is the little cousin of the Twins, though some people doubt the legitimacy of this familial connection.

K-SIN-153.content: Sammie is known to Mary from his childhood, specifically in relation to playing the guitar.

K-SIN-154.content: Sammie feels a need to assert his authority and connection to the Twins.

K-SIN-177.content: Sammie is a sharecropper from Sunflower Plantation, known as 'Preacherboy' because his father was a Pastor.

K-SIN-178.content: Sammie wrote a song for his father, revealing a deep personal connection to his music.

K-SIN-179.content: Sammie is capable of entering a trance-like state while playing guitar, connecting him to ancestral spirits.

K-SIN-203.content: Sammie possesses a beautiful singing voice that attracts attention.

K-SIN-218.content: Sammie believes being with the Smokestack Twins makes him feel like he is flying, indicating a sense of exhilaration and belonging.

K-SIN-220.content: Sammie identifies as a cousin to the Smokestack Twins and wants to leave the 'plantation' just like them, asserting his connection and desire for freedom.

K-SIN-239.content: Sammie is deeply shocked and unable to speak or process Stack's death.

K-SIN-240.content: Sammie believes he could have prevented Stack's death and questions the nature of the attack.

K-SIN-260.content: Sammie believes his music and 'sinnin'' are responsible for attracting the Devil and the current dangers.

K-SIN-317.content: Sammie struggles to fully abandon his past, particularly his connection to music, despite his father's demands.

K-SIN-335.content: Sammie is suffering from cirrhosis and has only a few months left to live.

K-SIN-336.content: Sammie has a deep connection to music, particularly the 'Delta way' of playing guitar.

K-SIN-337.content: Sammie experiences nightmares about a specific past 'night' that involved Stack and his brother.

C-SIN-17.name: Jedidiah

K-SIN-042.content: Jedidiah is Sammie's father and a devout man who reads from the Bible.

K-SIN-044.content: Jedidiah expects Sammie to use his musical talent for church services and to help with sermons.

K-SIN-052.content: Jedidiah believes Sammie is 'dancing with the devil' and warns him about the consequences of his choices.

K-SIN-053.content: Jedidiah is concerned about Sammie's well-being and his association with Smoke and Stack.

K-SIN-327.content: Jedidiah believes that music, specifically the guitar, can be a tool of the devil.

C-SIN-21.name: Lisa Chow

K-SIN-075.content: Lisa Chow is 12 years old and Chinese American.

C-SIN-22.name: Bo Chow

K-SIN-077.content: Bo Chow is Chinese American and in his 30s.

K-SIN-078.content: Bo Chow is the father of Lisa Chow and husband of Grace Chow.

K-SIN-079.content: Bo Chow runs the Delta Grocery Store.

K-SIN-080.content: Bo Chow is capable of supplying large quantities of food items like catfish and collards.

K-SIN-276.content: Bo Chow is a vampire who attempts to lure Grace out of the mill using a deceptive, charming demeanor.

K-SIN-292.content: Bo Chow can speak Cantonese and was in Grace's husband's mind.

K-SIN-293.content: Bo Chow is vulnerable to fire and ignites when pushed into a blaze.

C-SIN-22.imagePrompt: A man who is part of Remmick's group, capable of speaking Cantonese.

C-SIN-23.name: Terry

K-SIN-081.content: Terry is an acquaintance of Smoke.

K-SIN-082.content: Terry was shot in the ass cheek by Smoke.

K-SIN-083.content: Terry believed Smoke and his brother were still in Chicago working for Capone.

C-SIN-23.imagePrompt: A black man who is shot in the ass cheek, spins around, and complains about his injury.

C-SIN-24.name: Second Thief

K-SIN-084.content: The Second Thief is from Maybell.

C-SIN-25.name: Grace Chow

K-SIN-089.content: Grace Chow is Chinese American and in her 30s.

K-SIN-090.content: Grace Chow is the mother of Lisa Chow and wife of Bo Chow.

K-SIN-091.content: Grace Chow works at the Other Delta Grocery Store.

K-SIN-092.content: Grace is a shrewd businesswoman who can negotiate effectively for her services.

K-SIN-263.content: Grace is deeply concerned for her husband, Bo, and is willing to risk her own safety to find him.

K-SIN-281.content: Grace is mortified by Remmick's personal knowledge of her.

K-SIN-282.content: Grace is fiercely protective of her family and the town, willing to fight back.

K-SIN-283.content: Grace is capable of making a Molotov Cocktail and using it as a weapon.

C-SIN-25.imagePrompt: A determined woman, visibly distressed but quickly becoming resolute.

K-SIN-094.content: Delta Slim is a talented harmonica player who is also prone to drinking.

K-SIN-095.content: Delta Slim has a long-standing commitment to playing at Messengers every Saturday night.

K-SIN-096.content: Delta Slim is susceptible to the allure of good beer and higher pay.

K-SIN-128.content: Delta Slim has a history of being imprisoned for vagrancy and forced to perform music for white sheriffs.

K-SIN-129.content: Delta Slim believes white folks enjoy blues music but dislike the Black people who create it.

K-SIN-130.content: Delta Slim experienced trauma from his imprisonment, leading him to drink away his earnings.

K-SIN-142.content: Delta Slim believes the blues carries a sacred spirit, similar to religious faith but more honest.

K-SIN-143.content: Delta Slim views Africa as the 'home' from which the blues originated.

K-SIN-144.content: Delta Slim is willing to mentor younger musicians like Sammie.

K-SIN-237.content: Delta Slim is responsible for watching the door of the Juke Joint.

K-SIN-261.content: Delta Slim has past experience with vampires, having had a relationship with one.

K-SIN-270.content: Delta Slim exhibits a suspicious reaction to garlic, then attempts to cover it up with an excuse.

K-SIN-294.content: Delta Slim is willing to sacrifice himself to protect others.

K-SIN-295.content: Delta Slim can distract vampires with his singing and a broken bottle shard.

C-SIN-27.name: Pearline

K-SIN-158.content: Pearline has an older, 'church type' husband who is not present.

C-SIN-30.name: Mary

K-SIN-104.content: Mary was in a past romantic relationship with Stack, where he told her he loved her.

K-SIN-105.content: Mary's mother recently passed away, and she buried her yesterday.

K-SIN-106.content: Mary believes Stack and Smoke only loved her mother for what they could get from her.

K-SIN-173.content: Mary is now grown and no longer naive about Stack's intentions.

K-SIN-199.content: Mary is grieving, but remains observant and resourceful.

K-SIN-200.content: Mary believes she can extract information and money from the drifters more effectively than Stack.

K-SIN-201.content: Mary is willing to take personal risks to help the Juke Joint financially.

K-SIN-206.content: Mary is experiencing deep pain due to her mother's recent passing.

K-SIN-207.content: Mary is capable of using a pistol to defend herself.

K-SIN-334.content: Mary is aware of Stack's supernatural nature and is comfortable with it.

K-SIN-134.content: Cornbread is a sharecropper who is behind on his quota.

cd.outcome: Injured Man is rejected, Bert avoids danger.

dc.outcome: Injured Man exploits Bert's trust, Bert is harmed.

dd.outcome: Injured Man is rejected, Bert avoids exploitation.

cc.outcome: Injured Man finds temporary safety, Joan offers reluctant help.

cd.outcome: Injured Man is rejected, Joan avoids danger.

dc.outcome: Injured Man exploits Joan's trust, Joan is harmed.

dd.outcome: Injured Man is rejected, Joan avoids exploitation.

dc.outcome: Injured Man attacks, Chayton continues pursuit.

dd.outcome: Injured Man attacks, Chayton abandons search.

cc.outcome: Bert conceals, Chayton searches, but finds nothing.

dc.outcome: Bert reveals, Chayton captures Injured Man.

dd.outcome: Bert reveals, Chayton leaves, Injured Man is exposed.

cc.outcome: Joan conceals, Chayton searches, but finds nothing.

dc.outcome: Joan reveals, Chayton captures Injured Man.

dd.outcome: Joan reveals, Chayton leaves, Injured Man is exposed.

TK-SIN-001.content: The Injured Man seeks refuge, offering gold in exchange for sanctuary from his pursuers.

TK-SIN-002.content: The Injured Man kills Bert and reanimates him, revealing his demonic nature and the true danger.

T-SIN-02.description: What is the true nature of the gift of making music that can pierce the veil between life and death, and what are its consequences?

cc.outcome: Annie reveals the gift's nature, Injured Man demonstrates its dark side.

cd.outcome: Annie reveals, Injured Man conceals, leaving ambiguity.

dc.outcome: Annie withholds, Injured Man demonstrates, revealing danger.

dd.outcome: Annie withholds, Injured Man conceals, mystery persists.

TK-SIN-003.content: The Injured Man demonstrates the evil consequence of the gift by reanimating Bert, confirming Annie's warning.

participants.stake: learn and contribute to the venture

T-SIN-03.description: Can Smoke establish his juke joint and secure the necessary supplies and talent to make it a success in the Delta?

cc.outcome: Mill sold, terms accepted, uneasy truce established.

cd.outcome: Mill sold, Hogwood resists terms, future conflict likely.

dc.outcome: Smoke reveals weakness, Hogwood exploits, sale jeopardized.

dd.outcome: Both resist, sale fails, open conflict ensues.

TK-SIN-004.content: Smoke and Stack purchase the mill, equipment, and land, resolving the immediate acquisition.

TK-SIN-005.content: Smoke issues a direct threat to Hogwood, establishing dominance and warning against future interference.

TK-SIN-016.content: Smoke hires the Teenager to guard his truck, attempting to prevent disruptions to his supply acquisition.

TK-SIN-017.content: Smoke places a large order for event supplies with Bo Chow, initiating the acquisition process.

TK-SIN-018.content: Smoke's supply acquisition is violently interrupted by thieves, forcing him to defend his goods.

TK-SIN-019.content: Grace and Smoke negotiate a price for the signs, reaching an agreement.

TK-SIN-020.content: Stack uses beer and a higher offer to sway Delta Slim from his existing commitment.

TK-SIN-029.content: The montage shows the crew actively working together to transform the mill into a juke joint.

TK-SIN-030.content: Smoke guides the labor party and taps electricity, contributing to the juke joint's setup.

TK-SIN-031.content: Annie helps unload food and begins cooking, directly contributing to the juke joint's readiness.

TK-SIN-032.content: Sammie siphons water, loads equipment, and helps nail the stage, actively contributing to the setup.

TK-SIN-033.content: Grace Chow arrives with supplies and prepares the sign, aiding the juke joint's establishment.

TK-SIN-034.content: The Patron's attempt to pay with non-standard currency creates a direct challenge to the juke joint's business model.

T-SIN-04.description: Can Sammie establish himself as a blues player and find romantic connection in the Delta?

cc.outcome: Sammie plays church music, Jedidiah approves, tension remains.

cd.outcome: Sammie plays church music, Jedidiah allows, but disapproves.

dc.outcome: Sammie defies, Jedidiah enforces, open conflict.

TK-SIN-006.content: Sammie plays a blues version of a church song, directly challenging Jedidiah's expectations for worship.

TK-SIN-007.content: Jedidiah questions Sammie's musical choices and criticizes his desire for freedom over religious duty.

TK-SIN-021.content: Sammie flirts with Pearline, who responds playfully, initiating a potential romantic thread.

TK-SIN-035.content: Delta Slim calls Sammie over to help tune the piano, actively mentoring him in music.

TK-SIN-036.content: Sammie closely observes Slim and plays his guitar, demonstrating his eagerness to learn.

TK-SIN-043.content: Smoke challenges Sammie to perform well, setting a high expectation for his musical ability.

TK-SIN-044.content: Pearline dismisses Sammie's claim of being a cousin to the Twins, undermining his perceived status.

TK-SIN-045.content: Mary recognizes Sammie from his childhood, validating his past but challenging his current self-perception.

TK-SIN-054.content: Delta Slim enthusiastically introduces Sammie as a 'bad blues man' to the crowd.

TK-SIN-055.content: Sammie performs a deeply personal blues song, captivating the audience and revealing his identity.

TK-SIN-056.content: Smoke observes Sammie with surprise, while Stack watches with pride, acknowledging his talent.

TK-SIN-066.content: Smoke directly advises Sammie against a musician's life, highlighting its difficulties and suggesting he has other talents.

TK-SIN-112.content: An elderly Sammie performs blues with an electric guitar, indicating he has embraced music as part of his identity.

T-SIN-05.description: Can Sammie escape Jedidiah's influence and embrace a new life with Smoke and Stack, despite Jedidiah's warnings and the twins' dangerous lifestyle?

cc.outcome: Sammie leaves peacefully, Jedidiah accepts it.

cd.outcome: Sammie leaves, Jedidiah tries to stop him, creating conflict.

dc.outcome: Sammie stays, Jedidiah is content, Sammie is unhappy.

dd.outcome: Sammie stays reluctantly, Jedidiah is overbearing.

cc.outcome: Sammie integrates well, Smoke provides good guidance.

cd.outcome: Sammie integrates, but Smoke exposes him to danger.

dc.outcome: Sammie hesitates, Smoke guides him effectively.

dd.outcome: Sammie hesitates, Smoke exposes him to danger, creating tension.

cc.outcome: Sammie integrates well, Stack provides good mentorship.

cd.outcome: Sammie integrates, but Stack misleads or endangers him.

dc.outcome: Sammie hesitates, Stack mentors him effectively.

dd.outcome: Sammie hesitates, Stack misleads or endangers him, creating tension.

TK-SIN-008.content: Sammie ignores Jedidiah's warning and leaves with the twins, committing to a path away from his father's influence.

TK-SIN-009.content: Smoke questions Sammie's well-being and his father's treatment, showing concern and drawing Sammie into their world.

T-SIN-06.description: Will Sammie learn the truth about his family's past, specifically the circumstances surrounding Uncle Adam's death and the twins' involvement?

cd.outcome: Sammie asks, Stack conceals, creating frustration.

dc.outcome: Sammie remains silent, Stack reveals truth anyway.

dc.outcome: Sammie remains silent, Smoke reveals truth anyway.

dd.outcome: Sammie remains silent, Smoke conceals, truth remains hidden.

T-SIN-07.description: Will Sammie embrace his musical destiny and play like Charlie Patton?

T-SIN-08.description: Can Smoke maintain his reputation and authority in his hometown after returning from Chicago?

cd.outcome: Smoke's authority is questioned, Teenager is defiant.

TK-SIN-014.content: Smoke shoots Terry in the ass cheek, reasserting his dominance and punishing the attempted theft.

T-SIN-09.description: Can Stack escape his past entanglements and emotional baggage with Mary to move forward with his current life?

dc.outcome: Smoke demands compliance, Annie accepts, leading to a resentful but functional arrangement.

TK-SIN-023.content: Smoke proposes his juke joint venture to Annie, asking her to cook for it, initiating a potential partnership.

T-SIN-11.description: Can Smoke reconcile his pragmatic, materialist worldview with Annie's belief in spiritual practices and their shared past trauma?

cc.outcome: Smoke acknowledges spiritual value, Annie explains efficacy, leading to mutual understanding.

cd.outcome: Smoke acknowledges spiritual value, Annie condemns materialism, creating tension despite understanding.

dc.outcome: Smoke dismisses spiritual value, Annie explains efficacy, but Smoke remains unconvinced.

dd.outcome: Smoke dismisses spiritual value, Annie condemns materialism, resulting in a deep ideological rift.

TK-SIN-024.content: Smoke dismisses Annie's 'make believe' currency and spiritual beliefs, creating a direct conflict of worldviews.

TK-SIN-025.content: Annie challenges Smoke's materialism and questions his past actions, deepening the ideological rift between them.

T-SIN-13.description: Can Annie and Smoke overcome their past hurts and rebuild their relationship amidst the challenges of their lives?

cc.outcome: they reconnect deeply, healing old wounds

cd.outcome: Annie's openness is met with Smoke's hesitation

dc.outcome: Smoke's commitment is met with Annie's guardedness
